gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/236)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/236)
text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ وَ ذَكَرَ مِصْرَ فَقَالَ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَا تَأْكُلُوا فِي فَخَّارِهَا وَ لَا تَغْسِلُوا رُءُوسَكُمْ بِطِينِهَا فَإِنَّهُ يَذْهَبُ بِالْغَيْرَةِ وَ يُورِثُ الدِّيَاثَةَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moala Bin Muhammad, altogether from Ali Bin Asbaat,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying, and Egypt was mentioned, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Prophet<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Neither eat in its pottery, nor wash your heads with its clay, for it would remove the honour and inherit the actions of a pimp’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from and al-Husayn from Muhammad from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad all from Ali ibn Asbat who has said the following: “I once heard abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘When a city (Egypt) was mentioned, the Holy Prophet, said, ‘You must not eat in its pottery bowls; do not wash your head with its clay because it takes away one’s courage (protective feeling) and causes one to become a pimp.’”
